title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. neapolitana
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. neapolitana, also known as Italian gray sage, is a shrubby perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and yellow flower heads. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. neapolitana have been linked to several health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and are often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
